title: Exploring the electrophysiology of Parkinson's disease - magnetoencephalography combined with deep brain recordings from the subthalamic nucleus.
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous magnetoencephalography and subthalamic nucleus local field potential recordings from externalized deep brain stimulation patients with Parkinson's disease, acquired during rest, static motor tasks, and self-paced movement under both medicated and unmedicated states. The study investigates basal ganglia-cortex circuit electrophysiology and its modulation by motor behavior and dopaminergic medication.
methods_description: Data were collected from externalized DBS patients undergoing simultaneous MEG-STN LFP recordings with and without dopaminergic medication. Two movement conditions were employed: (1) 5 minutes of rest followed by static forearm extension, and (2) 5 minutes of rest followed by self-paced fist-clenching. Some patients were recorded in resting-state only.

funding_references.funder_name: The project was funded by ERA-NET Neuron for 3 years.
